name: As Paxareiras Loop
description: This difficult 27.3-mile touring cycling loop in Galicia offers challenging climbs, coastal views, and panoramic vistas from viewpoints.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Muros offer a diverse landscape characterized by its Atlantic coastline, estuaries, and varied inland terrain. The region features a mix of paved roads and paths, with options ranging from flat coastal stretches to routes with significant elevation changes. This area of Galicia provides a setting for cycling that includes both sea views and green landscapes. Best touring cycling routes around Muros The most popular touring cycling route is As Paxareiras Loop, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route features challenging climbs and panoramic vistas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of the Coast – View of Faro Punta Insua loop from Louro, a moderate 13.9 miles (22.3 km) path. This route offers coastal views and passes by Faro Punta Insua. Local touring cyclists also love the Coastal Road to Muros – Playa de Medrón loop from Muros, a 45.3 miles (73.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Muros is defined by coastal paths, green landscapes, and routes with significant climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging loops. The routes in Muros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Muros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I expect in Muros?
A: Muros offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists, ranging from coastal paths with stunning sea views to green inland routes. You'll find a mix of mostly paved roads and paths, catering to various preferences. The region is known for both moderate rides and challenging loops with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Muros provides options for different ability levels. While some routes feature challenging climbs, there are also moderate paths available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31702344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Coast – View of Faro Punta Insua loop from Louro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.9-mile route offering beautiful coastal views without extreme difficulty.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Muros's touring cycling routes often pass by significant natural attractions and landmarks. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4803815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3859881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moa Summit (Monte Pindo)&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4734193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1644912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Beach, O Pindo&lt;/a&gt;, and the natural monument of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Louro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Muros?
A: The most popular months for touring cycling in Muros are August and September. During these months, the weather is generally favorable for exploring the region's diverse landscapes, from its Atlantic coastline to its green inland areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Muros?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Muros are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955969863/as-paxareiras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Paxareiras Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Road to Muros – Playa de Medrón loop from Muros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Muros?
A: The touring cycling routes in Muros are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from stunning coastal views to challenging climbs, and the overall natural beauty of the region.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Muros is renowned for its picturesque coastline, and many cycling paths offer stunning coastal views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31702344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Coast – View of Faro Punta Insua loop from Louro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38251944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Road to Muros – Vouga Beach loop from Monte Naraio&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for experiencing the Atlantic shoreline.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the historic town of Muros?
A: While specific routes might vary, Muros itself boasts a charming historic old town with a lively fishing port and narrow alleys. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Road to Muros – Playa de Medrón loop from Muros&lt;/a&gt; start and end in Muros, allowing you to explore the town before or after your ride.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling in Muros?
A: Muros offers a range of difficulties. You'll find moderate routes suitable for a good fitness level, as well as more challenging loops with significant climbs and elevations. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955969863/as-paxareiras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Paxareiras Loop&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult due to its climbs and panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there options for longer touring cycling adventures?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Muros has routes that cover significant distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Road to Muros – Playa de Medrón loop from Muros&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 45.3-mile trail, offering a comprehensive tour of the coastal areas and taking approximately 4 hours and 44 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain in Muros is varied, featuring a mix of paved roads and paths. You'll encounter flat coastal stretches, rolling hills, and some routes with significant elevation changes, particularly in the inland areas. This diversity ensures a dynamic cycling experience.
tips: Panorama point indicated on the map and with a landscaped parking lot.

Panorama point over Ría de Muros and Noya
The estuary of Muros and Noya (in Galician, ría de Muros e Noia), also known as ría de Muros or ría de Noya, is part of the rías Bajas and is located between the Corcubión estuary in the north and the estuary of Arosa in the south. It is located in the province of La Coruña, and the Barbanza Peninsula delimits its coasts to the south. The River Tambre flows into this estuary.

The waters of the Tambre are those that originate at the confluence with the sea, a habitat in which bivalves, cockles and cockles develop, which are the economic engine of the peoples that surround it.

The waters of the estuary starts from La Aguieira Beach in Puerto del Son, on the south coast, and ends at San Francisco Beach, in Louro, on the north coast. Among these are the beaches of As Gaivotas (in Miñortos), Testal (Noya), Broña (Outes), Medrón and Parameán (Esteiro), Cabanas (Tal) and O Castelo (Muros), among others.

Among the villages to highlight are those that give name to the estuary: Muros, was founded behind Mount Louro as a fishing village hidden from the sight of pirates and corsairs; Noya, medieval village par excellence; other towns are Puerto del Son and Portosín.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/R%C3%ADa_de_Muros_y_Noya
The Louredo viewpoint on the Galician Atlantic coast located above Carnota. Hard climb but worth it

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the As Paxareiras Loop by touring bicycle?
faqs.answer: The As Paxareiras Loop is designed for touring bicycles and generally takes around 4 hours and 24 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is the As Paxareiras Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the As Paxareiras Loop is rated as difficult and is best suited for intermediate to experienced touring cyclists. It features a significant climb with an average gradient of 6.2% to 6.43% over 8 kilometers, including sections that reach 13.5%.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the As Paxareiras Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily features a challenging climb, characteristic of the As Paxareiras area. You'll encounter a good mix of steep and more manageable sections, allowing for some recovery during the ascent. Expect varied road surfaces typical of mountainous and rural regions in Galicia.

faqs.question: What viewpoints or landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers several scenic spots. You can enjoy panoramic views from the Viewpoint of the Muros and Noia estuary, another general Viewpoint, a dedicated View of the Coast, and the Mirador de Louredo (Carnota). These provide extensive vistas of the Atlantic coast and surrounding rural landscapes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the As Paxareiras Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is typically during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder, with less extreme heat or heavy rainfall, making the challenging climbs more enjoyable and the coastal and mountainous views clearer.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the As Paxareiras Loop?
faqs.answer: As a loop route, you can typically start and park in various locations around the Muros or Carnota areas. While there isn't one designated 'trailhead parking' explicitly mentioned, look for public parking in the towns or villages along the route, ensuring it's suitable for leaving your vehicle for several hours.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle the As Paxareiras Loop, especially within ZEC Carnota - Monte Pindo?
faqs.answer: The As Paxareiras Loop passes through the ZEC Carnota - Monte Pindo area. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within this protected zone. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the As Paxareiras Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on roads suitable for touring bicycles, if you plan to stop or explore off-road sections, it's important to note that dogs are generally allowed in the ZEC Carnota - Monte Pindo area. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially near wildlife or livestock, and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Does the As Paxareiras Loop intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will cycle along parts of the [CIMA AC07] As Paxareiras * Abelleira, [CIMA AC07] As Paxareiras * O Viso - Carnota, and [CIMA AC07] As Paxareiras * Sestaio. Smaller segments also include Camiño á Praia da Arca and Ponte dos Muíños.

faqs.question: What should I pack for touring cycling the As Paxareiras Loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and duration, pack essentials like sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, appropriate cycling attire for varying weather, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A fully charged phone with GPS capabilities is also highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or water along the As Paxareiras Loop?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through rural areas, you may find small cafes or shops in the villages of Muros or Carnota, or other smaller settlements along the way. However, it's always best to carry enough water and snacks for the entire duration, especially during the challenging climb, as services can be sparse.
